body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
Closes #1242 

Extract most of the code out of the `partitioned_turbulent_fluxes!` function and move it into a function that dispatches on the component model type. For atmosphere models, the function does nothing, and for surface models it performs the flux calculation that was previously in `partitioned_turbulent_fluxes!`.

This design allows us to dispatch on model type instead of checking the type with an `if` statement, and, more importantly, it allows us to extend `compute_surface_fluxes!` for surface models where we want to perform different calculations (e.g. the integrated land model). 


## Content
- [x] Add `compute_surface_fluxes!` function, write methods for atmosphere and surface models
- [x] Write method for integrated land model - requires #1199 and https://github.com/CliMA/ClimaLand.jl/pull/1062
- [x] Make `import_atmos_fields` depend on surface model type, since exchange variables depend on surface model type
- [x] Add a test to compute surface fluxes between integrated land and ClimaAtmos, and check that fluxes stored in the coupler are changed and non-zero

### Dependency updates
This PR requires ClimaLand v0.15.13, so it updates the dependencies. The changes include:
```
    Updating `~/clima/ClimaCoupler.jl/experiments/ClimaEarth/Project.toml`
  [d414da3d] ↑ ClimaCore v0.14.28 ⇒ v0.14.29
  [08f4d4ce] ↑ ClimaLand v0.15.12 ⇒ v0.15.13
  [0bca4576] ↑ SciMLBase v2.79.0 ⇒ v2.81.0
```


<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

- **New Features**
  - Enhanced simulation integration with improved handling of atmospheric composition and moisture fluxes.
  - Introduced new functionality for updating simulation fields and computing surface fluxes.
  - Added a new configuration file to streamline test setups and parameter tuning.

- **Chores**
  - Updated runtime and dependency versions to boost performance and compatibility.

- **Tests**
  - Expanded test coverage with new scenarios verifying simulation flux calculations.
  - Introduced a new test set for "ClimaLandSimulation flux calculations".

- **Refactor**
  - Streamlined code for simulation field exchanges and flux computations, enhancing overall reliability.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
title: add compute_surface_fluxes function for component models; test land fluxes

body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
Document the default coupler fields, so it's more clear for users whether they need to add extra coupler exchange fields or not.

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

- **Documentation**
  - Revised descriptions and measurement units for energy and water coupler fields to better express a vertically integrated per surface area perspective.
  - Added a list of default coupler exchange fields, including their names, descriptions, and units.
  
- **New Features**
  - Introduced functionality to initialize default coupler exchange fields with the flexibility to extend them for various simulation components.
  - Added a method to update the fields of the component model simulation influenced by the coupler.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
title: document coupler fields

body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
closes #1241

A lot of files are modified in this PR but I kept each change as a separate commit so they might be easier to review.

## Content
- [x] remove `domain_type` argument to land constructors
- [x] remove `CoupledSimulation.dates.date` - can replace with `current_date(t)`
  - how do we do this in `checkpoint_sims`? We don't have access to `t`
  - to get around this, I added `t` to the `CoupledSimulation` struct. This is quite similar to storing `dates.date` in the `CoupledSimulation`, but to me it seems more logical to store `t` since that's what we use to step the simulation. It also prevents us from having to pass `cs, t` to some functions
- [x] remove `domain` from SurfaceModelSimulations (never used)
- [x] remove `TimeManager.strdate_to_datetime` (never used)
- [x] simplify FT access in tests


<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

- **Refactor**
  - Consolidated time management by replacing multiple date parameters with a single, unified start date.
  - Removed redundant simulation configuration parameters to streamline initialization and callback triggers.

- **Documentation**
  - Updated user documentation to reflect the simplified interfaces and removal of outdated functions.

- **Tests**
  - Revised test setups for enhanced consistency with the updated simulation components.
  - Updated test cases to reflect changes in constructor signatures and parameter handling.
  - Removed tests associated with the TimeManager functionality.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
title: misc cleanup
